questions.q: The question 'which AI model is best?' is best reframed as:
choices: Which tier fits the workload I'm doing?
Which model has the highest benchmark score?
Which model did a YouTuber rank highest?
questions.explain: Capability is workload-relative. The 'best' model for a hard multi-step refactor is different from the 'best' model for summarising 10,000 customer reviews. Tier mapping beats leaderboard chasing.

questions.q: Which tier is the right default for 'daily Q&A, drafting, code review, meeting notes'?
questions.explain: Tier 3 — Daily-Driver. Models like Kimi K2.7, Qwen 3.5, and MiniMax M3 handle 80% of routine work for a fraction of frontier cost.

questions.q: You need to refactor a multi-file migration in production, with tests. Which tier should you reach for first?
questions.explain: Hard agentic coding lives in Tier 2 — Sonnet 4.7, GPT 5.4, Gemini 3 Pro. Near-frontier at 1/3 to 1/5 the cost of Opus. Only escalate to Tier 1 if Tier 2 fails the task.

questions.q: Short answer: name one workload that belongs in Tier 4, and why you'd pick Tier 4 over Tier 3 for it.
questions.explain: Any of: bulk translation across thousands of strings; batch extraction from 50,000 customer reviews; classifying a million support tickets; processing sensitive data you don't want leaving your laptop (local 7B-20B). The reason: Tier 4 is designed for volume, not peak quality. At scale, 'good enough' compounds into a real saving — or in the privacy case, it's the only option.

questions.q: Per the source video, what does 'S-tier' actually mean on a model leaderboard?
choices: The best model for the workload the tier-list creator tested.
The model with the highest context window.
questions.explain: S-tier means 'best for what the creator tested.' It does not mean 'best for everything' or 'best for your workload' unless your workload matches the creator's. This is the S-tier trap.

questions.q: True or false: 'Frontier models are smarter than daily-driver models in an absolute sense.' Explain in one sentence.
questions.explain: False. Frontier models are tuned for a different shape of problem — long-horizon reasoning, novel architecture, complex multi-step work. On routine summarisation or extraction, a daily-driver model often matches them. The difference is workload-shape, not absolute IQ.
